Cabin Management Systems.clue: This power-regulation LRU lives in the electrical system, even though a tech may mistake it for a CMS box.
Cabin Management Systems.explanation: The generator control unit regulates bus voltage and is part of the aircraft electrical system, not the CMS.

Cabin Management Systems.clue: Doing this before actually diagnosing an IFE fault turns troubleshooting into parts roulette.
choices: Confirming the pilot report with the crew
Swapping LRUs before diagnosing the fault
Reading fault codes from the diagnostic port
Verifying power and ground at the connector
Cabin Management Systems.explanation: Systematic troubleshooting isolates the fault before replacing parts; shotgun-swapping LRUs violates the methodology.

Cabin Management Systems.clue: Skipping this save step before a CMS load can erase the owner's cabin settings for good.
Cabin Management Systems.explanation: Without a pre-load configuration backup, a failed or interrupted update can permanently lose customer settings.

Cabin Management Systems.clue: Running CMS cabling right next to these switching boxes bathes data lines in transient EMI.
Cabin Management Systems.explanation: Relay switching produces fast voltage transients that couple into nearby conductors, increasing EMI on CMS lines.

Cabin Management Systems.clue: Running BIT on only cabin lighting leaves these other CMS areas still in the dark.
Cabin Management Systems.explanation: Testing a single subsystem leaves IFE, A/V, seat, and climate subsystems unverified, which is why the check is incomplete.

Cabin Management Systems.clue: Leaving these radios turned on even though no one uses them quietly widens the attack surface.
choices: A unique SSID with a strong password
Cabin Management Systems.explanation: Enabling unused wireless features gives attackers additional entry points without adding cabin functionality.

Cabin Management Systems.clue: An expired one of these affects the FMS, not a blank CMS screen.
Cabin Management Systems.explanation: Nav database expiration affects FMS navigation capability; it has nothing to do with CMS display operation.

Cabin Management Systems.clue: With the breaker set and the screen dark, you grab a DMM and read this first.
choices: Data bus traffic on the CMS network
Touch calibration at the screen face
Installed software version in the menu
Bus voltage at the display power connector
Cabin Management Systems.explanation: Before chasing data or software, confirm the display is actually receiving bus voltage at its power connector.
